title: dianthus × saxatilis
intro: Dianthus × saxatilis, also known as the Fringed Pink, is a hybrid perennial plant belonging to the carnation family, characterized by its delicate, fragrant flowers and slender foliage. Traditionally, the plant's flowers and leaves have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, due to their mild anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dianthus × saxatilis include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for skin conditions and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
